title: reseda undata subsp. leucantha
intro: Reseda undata subsp. leucantha, also known as white flowered reseda or white rod weed, is a plant species within the family Boraginaceae. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been known to provide health benefits such as relief from skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
